meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Solve Code Errors
content.seo_description: Learn how to debug code faster using AI. A beginner-level guide for freelancers comparing Antigravity and Claude Code to solve production bottlenecks.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at stack traces. By leveraging AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate root-cause analysis, generate precise patches, and verify fixes in seconds. Stop debugging manually—shift your workflow to an autonomous, AI-assisted paradigm that turns hours of troubleshooting into minutes of high-velocity development.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red error messages. You’ve been staring at the same block of logic for forty minutes, and the 'can't debug code' paralysis has fully set in. It’s the silent killer of freelance velocity—the moment your billable hours turn into unpaid frustration.

But the era of manual debugging is over. Modern AI agents don't just suggest syntax fixes; they understand architectural intent, context, and edge cases. By integrating tools like Antigravity and Claude Code into your local environment, you move from 'troubleshooter' to 'architect,' letting AI handle the heavy lifting while you maintain total creative control.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Before running AI, you must define the scope. Narrow down the error to a specific function or module to prevent the AI from hallucinating across your entire codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this file and identify the exact line causing the runtime exception. Explain the root cause in plain English.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ls && claude ask 'Find the runtime error in [filename]'

steps.title: Contextualize with Logs
steps.description: AI needs data to be effective. Feed the relevant logs and the error stack trace into the tool to provide a baseline for the fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Here are the error logs. Cross-reference them with the current logic and propose a fix that maintains existing state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --run 'cat error.log' && claude ask 'Fix the bug based on these logs'

steps.title: Simulate and Test
steps.description: Use AI to generate a unit test that recreates the bug, ensuring the fix is actually verified before you commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a unit test for this function that forces a failure when the bug is present.

steps.title: Apply Refactored Patch
steps.description: Apply the suggested fix, ensuring the code style matches your current repository standards.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Apply the fix to the function, ensuring you follow existing clean code patterns and error handling standards.

steps.title: Automated Regression Audit
steps.description: Run a final scan to ensure the fix hasn't introduced side effects in related modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a dependency analysis on this module to ensure this fix didn't break downstream functionality.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Run regression tests and analyze potential side effects'

pro_tips: Always use the '--verbose' flag in CLI tools to see exactly which files the AI is scanning; it prevents 'context bloat'.
If the AI struggles, feed it the 'package.json' first; AI often fails because of version mismatches you didn't realize existed.
Treat AI as a junior developer: ask it to explain its reasoning before it writes code to avoid 'lazy' patches.
Use a dedicated 'debug-scratchpad.md' file to keep the AI focused on current task context, clearing it once the bug is resolved.
Never blindly accept a fix; ask the AI to 'critique its own solution for performance bottlenecks' before applying.
content.next_steps: Once your bug is squashed, document the fix in your project's README or wiki to prevent future occurrences, and consider setting up an automated CI/CD pipeline to catch similar errors on push.
content.conversion_block: Ready to stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
